task_text:
Read vendor_contacts.csv and write the names and renewal dates of any vendors
with a contract renewal in July 2026 to a new file called "july_renewals.txt".

content.text: I'll start by reading the CSV file to see its contents!

content.content:
Vendor,Contact,Email,Phone,Contract Renewal
Acme Supplies,Robert Chen,r.chen@acme.example.com,555-0142,2026-09-01
TechParts Inc.,Sandra Okafor,s.okafor@techparts.example.com,555-0287,2026-08-15
Global Print Co.,Wei Zhang,w.zhang@globalprint.example.com,555-0394,2026-11-30
FastShip Logistics,Anika Patel,a.patel@fastship.example.com,555-0521,2026-07-31
CleanSpace Janitorial,Tom Brennan,t.brennan@cleanspace.example.com,555-0618,2026-10-15

content.text: I can see the data. Only **FastShip Logistics** has a renewal in July 2026 (2026-07-31). Now I'll write that to the new file.

input.content:
Vendors with Contract Renewals in July 2026:

FastShip Logistics - 2026-07-31

content.text:
Done! Here's a summary of what I did:

1. **Read** `vendor_contacts.csv` — found 5 vendors and their contract renewal dates.
2. **Identified** the one vendor with a July 2026 renewal: **FastShip Logistics** (2026-07-31).
3. **Wrote** that vendor's name and renewal date to the new file `july_renewals.txt`.
